mcq.question: What is the purpose of storage classes in C?
options: Control scope, lifetime, and visibility of variables

mcq.question: Which storage class is the default for local variables?
Which keyword is used for automatic variables?
Where are auto variables stored?
What is the lifetime of an auto variable?
Which storage class preserves a variable's value between function calls?
What is the default value of a static variable?
Which storage class requests storage in CPU registers?
Why are register variables used?
Which storage class is used to access global variables from another file?
What does extern indicate?
Which storage class has global lifetime?
Which variable exists throughout program execution?
Can the address of a register variable always be obtained?
Which storage class limits visibility to the current file?
Which storage class is commonly used for loop counters?
Storage classes determine?
A static local variable is initialized only?
Which storage class keyword is optional for local variables?
Why are storage classes important?

true_false.statement: auto is the default storage class for local variables.
Static variables retain their values between function calls.
Register variables are stored in CPU registers whenever possible.
Extern variables are local to a function.
Static variables have a default value of 0.
Auto variables exist throughout program execution.
The address of a register variable may not be accessible.
Extern is used for sharing global variables across files.
Static variables are initialized every time a function is called.

fill_blanks.question: The default storage class for local variables is ________.
The ________ storage class retains values between function calls.
The ________ storage class requests CPU register storage.
The ________ keyword is used to access global variables defined in another file.
Storage classes control variable scope and ________.
Auto variables are generally stored on the ________.
Register variables are used for ________ access.
